mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during expansion regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with p-values exceeding 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 70% of months where inflation crosses the 0.4 percent threshold, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales validates sustainable labor-market driven consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform baseline returns, or yields negative outcomes in more than 50% of co-occurring months during out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment levels above 7 percent indicate structural labor market weakness, suppressing near-term equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of observations during confirmed recessionary periods, or loses predictive power when unemployment exceeds 9 percent.
